title: A Funny Thing Happened On The Way To The

jokes.text: Really? I hadn't noticed.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey has a dart stuck in his forehead and someone points it out
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's bone-dry sarcastic response to the obvious
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey deadpan delivery - could only come from his character. Excellent economy and structure.

jokes.text: You people don't pay me enough for this.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey is 'dead' in their murder mystery game
jokes.punchline: He breaks character to complain about his job
jokes.explanation: Classic Geoffrey complaining about the family. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: Then you couldn't possibly feel me do this.
jokes.setup: Someone tells Geoffrey to be quiet because he's dead
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey retorts that if he's dead, he can't feel whatever physical action he's doing
jokes.explanation: Good logic-based humor with implied physical comedy.

jokes.text: Honey, I'm home. Hey, Lucy.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil enters the house
jokes.punchline: He references I Love Lucy, calling Vivian 'Lucy'
jokes.explanation: Basic TV reference humor. Gets a chuckle but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Yeah, and George Bush is really looking forward to playing with his grandchildren.
jokes.setup: Will protests his innocence
jokes.punchline: Carlton's sarcastic political reference (Bush recently lost election)
jokes.explanation: Timely political humor that fits Carlton's character. Good sarcastic structure.

jokes.text: But I bet you do, right?
jokes.setup: Will turns to address someone directly
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall to audience
jokes.explanation: Classic Fresh Prince fourth-wall break. Very characteristic of the show.

jokes.text: Good, Aunt Vivian. Good, Aunt Vivian. Here, beg for a Scooby Snack.
jokes.setup: Vivian is doing breathing exercises on all fours
jokes.punchline: Will treats her like a dog doing tricks
jokes.explanation: Good visual observation with pop culture reference. Typical Will irreverence.

jokes.text: I don't know, Aunt Viv. Seem to me all that heavy breathing is what got you in this mess in the first place.
jokes.setup: Vivian explains she's practicing breathing for Lamaze
jokes.punchline: Will implies the breathing is sexual in nature
jokes.explanation: Excellent double entendre with perfect setup/payoff structure. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Still hurts.
jokes.setup: Vivian points out his dog died 20 years ago
jokes.punchline: Phil's deadpan commitment to the ridiculous excuse
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery. Maximum comedy from minimal words. Pure character.

jokes.text: Hey, hey, hey. Come on, Aunt Viv. Now this is the most straight-up, honest dude I know. The day that he lies to you, may all my hair fall out.
jokes.setup: Will vouches for Phil's honesty with a dramatic oath
jokes.punchline: Immediately followed by Phil faking a back injury
jokes.explanation: Excellent dramatic irony setup that pays off immediately. Well-earned comedy.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, is my hairline receding?
jokes.setup: Phil immediately lies, triggering Will's oath about losing his hair
jokes.punchline: Will frantically checks his hairline
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the setup. Excellent structure and character consistency.

jokes.text: Freeze, bubble-butt!
jokes.setup: Vivian catches Phil celebrating
jokes.punchline: Her unexpectedly playful insult
jokes.explanation: Unexpected term of endearment/insult from Vivian adds levity to confrontation.

jokes.text: Good answer, Admiral Stockdale.
jokes.setup: Phil gives a non-answer when confronted
jokes.punchline: Reference to Ross Perot's running mate known for confusing debate performance
jokes.explanation: Sharp political reference to a memorable debate moment. Timely and clever.

jokes.text: Pop a Midol.
jokes.setup: Vivian snaps at Geoffrey after he brings her milk
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's blunt suggestion for her mood
jokes.explanation: Typical Geoffrey bluntness but fairly predictable PMS joke.

jokes.text: Sorry, homework. - Tennis lesson. I just don't want to.
jokes.setup: Vivian asks which kid will be her Lamaze coach
jokes.punchline: The kids give increasingly honest excuses, ending with Ashley's brutal honesty
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from polite excuses to brutal honesty. Ashley's directness is perfect.

jokes.text: Look, Aunt Viv, if I was one of your kids... I'd be short with a square head...
jokes.setup: Will volunteers to help Vivian
jokes.punchline: Roasts Carlton and the family genetics
jokes.explanation: Classic Will roasting Carlton's appearance. Good visual comedy and character dynamics.

jokes.text: Well, that's cool. Go, Vivian. Get busy, have a baby.
jokes.setup: Will agrees to coach Vivian
jokes.punchline: His casual, hip-hop influenced encouragement for childbirth
jokes.explanation: Will's street attitude applied to childbirth coaching. Character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Man, she is all that. She's fine. She's one of The Fat Boys.
jokes.setup: Will sees an attractive woman and praises her
jokes.punchline: Reveals she's heavily pregnant by comparing to rap group
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with cultural reference. Classic Will perspective on attraction vs reality.

jokes.text: Hey, you know, can you do me a favor? I have not seen my feet since Lent. Can you tell me, do my shoes match? No. Damn.
jokes.setup: Danny asks Vivian to check if her shoes match
jokes.punchline: They don't match, frustrating her further
jokes.explanation: Good pregnancy humor with realistic frustration. Well-structured with payoff.

jokes.text: No, he's in Mexico. - On vacation? No, on his secretary. But don't worry, he's not having a good time. I filled his thong with BenGay.
jokes.setup: Danny explains her husband won't show up to Lamaze class
jokes.punchline: Reveals affair and her revenge with BenGay in his thong
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with unexpected revenge twist. Great structure and quotable punchline.

jokes.text: You're the Danny Mitchell? Wait, hold it. I heard Charles Barkley on the radio talking about... him and Danny was gonna rumble in the parking lot... after the Laker game. He won't show up, that wuss.
jokes.setup: Will discovers Danny is the famous sportswriter he admires
jokes.punchline: Danny casually calls Charles Barkley a wuss
jokes.explanation: Good subversion - tough sportswriter is pregnant woman. Celebrity reference works well.

jokes.text: You need some help there, Danny? - Why, you got a forklift?
jokes.setup: Will offers to help pregnant Danny get positioned
jokes.punchline: Danny's self-deprecating forklift joke
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating pregnancy humor. Excellent economy in delivery.

jokes.text: No, you don't. You just don't want to sleep on the couch, Philip. I hate sleeping on that thing. It's all lumpy.
jokes.setup: Phil claims he came to class out of responsibility
jokes.punchline: Vivian calls him out, Phil immediately admits it's about the couch
jokes.explanation: Good marital dynamics humor. Phil's immediate honesty about selfish motives.

jokes.text: It doesn't sound miraculous. It sounds yucky.
jokes.setup: Vivian describes childbirth as miraculous
jokes.punchline: Phil's childish assessment
jokes.explanation: Phil's manchild reaction to childbirth. Simple but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: You should have thought about that seven months ago... before that second bottle of wine, Philip.
jokes.setup: Phil complains about childbirth being yucky
jokes.punchline: Vivian reveals the conception circumstances
jokes.explanation: Good reveal about their romantic life with specific detail. Realistic marital humor.

jokes.text: Are they always like this? - No, sometimes they fight.
jokes.setup: Danny asks if Phil and Vivian always bicker
jokes.punchline: Will deadpans that this IS their good behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan structure. Great economy and character observation.

jokes.text: It was hype. Like, Worthy passes to Vlade. Vlade back to Byron Scott. Byron Scott back to Vlade. Vlade gets the ball back to Worthy. Worthy gets it, stuffs, pop. The Lakers win the game!
jokes.setup: Will describes the exciting basketball game
jokes.punchline: His animated play-by-play recreation
jokes.explanation: Will's enthusiasm for sports shown through animated storytelling - good character moment

jokes.text: You know what the high point of the game was for me, huh? What? When you punched Jack Nicholson in the nose for touching your stomach?
jokes.setup: Will asks about the high point of the game for Danny
jokes.punchline: References her punching Jack Nicholson for touching her belly
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity reference with pregnant woman's boundary issues. Specific and visual.

jokes.text: Indigestion. I guess I shouldn't have had that third chili dog, huh?
jokes.setup: Expected her to agree about punching Jack Nicholson
jokes.punchline: She was just having indigestion from too much food
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection but fairly predictable pregnant woman food humor.

jokes.text: My water broke. That's cool. We got another one, right here in the fridge.
jokes.setup: Danny announces her water broke
jokes.punchline: Tony misunderstands, offers replacement water bottle
jokes.explanation: Classic misunderstanding humor but fairly predictable. Gets the job done.

jokes.text: I just had the car cleaned. Can't you hold it for a couple of hours?
jokes.setup: Danny is about to give birth in the limo
jokes.punchline: Tony's concern for his car's cleanliness over emergency childbirth
jokes.explanation: Good character priorities humor. Tony's misplaced concern is relatable and funny.

jokes.text: Hey, I have feelings, you know.
jokes.setup: Will calls Tony an idiot for his car concerns
jokes.punchline: Tony's hurt feelings response during emergency
jokes.explanation: Tony's misplaced priorities continue. Decent character moment but not exceptional.

jokes.text: It's kind of like trying to squeeze Della Reese out of a Hyundai!
jokes.setup: Will asks how Danny's doing in labor
jokes.punchline: Vivid comparison using celebrity and car size disparity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual metaphor. Original, specific, and perfectly captures the situation.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. They're 10 seconds apart. You're having a baby. You think?
jokes.setup: Will times contractions and announces she's having a baby
jokes.punchline: Danny's sarcastic response to the obvious
jokes.explanation: Basic sarcasm to obvious statement. Functional but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: Well, I hope you're all happy. Now I'm gonna have a big, ugly, red circle in the middle of my forehead. I'll look just like Carlton's yearbook picture.
jokes.setup: Hilary got hit with dart during murder mystery game
jokes.punchline: Compares her dart mark to Carlton's appearance
jokes.explanation: Good callback that connects to earlier Carlton roasting. Hilary's vanity focus is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I'm going through all this in the back of a limo... because of what went on in the back of my Jeep.
jokes.setup: Danny reflects on her situation
jokes.punchline: Connects current birth location to conception location
jokes.explanation: Good connection between conception and birth circumstances. Nice structural parallel.

jokes.text: Wow, I got to get me a Jeep.
jokes.setup: Danny mentions what happened in her Jeep
jokes.punchline: Will's teenage reaction to sexual implication
jokes.explanation: Perfect teenage Will reaction. Character-specific and well-timed.

jokes.text: I don't want to drink it. I want you to smack me over the head with it! - What? - Just until I pass out.
jokes.setup: Danny asks Will to get champagne, he assumes she wants to drink it
jokes.punchline: She wants to be knocked unconscious to avoid the pain
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with escalating desperation. Good structure and unexpected solution.

jokes.text: This baby's on its way, and I think it's bringing luggage.
jokes.setup: Danny describing how the baby feels
jokes.punchline: Metaphor about baby bringing luggage
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor for labor pain. Original and vivid imagery.

jokes.text: If I run really fast... I can get back to the Forum and have my baby on the floor... of a ladies' room with some dignity.
jokes.setup: Danny wants to escape the limo situation
jokes.punchline: She thinks a public bathroom would be more dignified
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic about dignity in impossible situations. Solid escalation.

jokes.text: I broke a nail. What the hell do you think?
jokes.setup: Doctor asks what the problem is with woman clearly in labor
jokes.punchline: Danny's sarcastic response with obvious understatement
jokes.explanation: Standard sarcasm to obvious question. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: I was looking for a doctor, and I found an M.D. License plate. I yanked the guy out of his car, and then I realized M.D. Stands for Mike Ditka.
jokes.setup: Tony explains how he found a doctor
jokes.punchline: He confused football coach Mike Ditka's initials for Medical Doctor
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection with specific cultural reference. Good structure and logical absurdity.

jokes.text: I got to make a phone call. I suddenly miss my mother.
jokes.setup: Danny is about to give birth
jokes.punchline: Labor makes her suddenly sentimental about family
jokes.explanation: Realistic emotional response to childbirth. Good character moment but not exceptional humor.

jokes.text: Focus? I don't want to focus. I want drugs! Now, get out in that parking lot and score!
jokes.setup: Will tries to help Danny focus during labor
jokes.punchline: She demands he get drugs from the parking lot
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of desperation. Danny's tough character asking for street drugs is funny.

jokes.text: Danny, think of it as the World Series, all right? All right, Alomar is rounding third. Here comes the throw. - Slide, Danny, slide.
jokes.setup: Will coaches Danny through labor using sports metaphors
jokes.punchline: He tells her to 'slide' like in baseball
jokes.explanation: Creative coaching technique using Danny's sportswriter background. Good character integration.

jokes.text: You want to hold him? No. No, thanks. I'll wait till somebody run him through a car wash or something.
jokes.setup: Doctor offers to let Danny hold her newborn
jokes.punchline: She wants the baby cleaned like a car first
jokes.explanation: Realistic new mother reaction with car wash metaphor fitting the limo setting.

jokes.text: Get busy. Put your thing down. It's your birthday.
jokes.setup: Will is trying to calm the crying newborn
jokes.punchline: He talks to the baby like he's at a party
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will response - treating baby like he's at a hip-hop party. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: As you know, Will, I have a separation in my lower lumbar area... and the couch is perfect to align my... - She threw you out, huh? You got that right.
jokes.setup: Phil gives elaborate medical explanation for sleeping on couch
jokes.punchline: Will cuts through BS, Phil immediately admits the truth
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamics. Will seeing through Phil's elaborate excuse, Phil's immediate honesty.

jokes.text: I really want to be in the room when you give birth. Not just because you want me there... but because I want to be the first person our baby sees upside down.
jokes.setup: Phil decides he wants to be at the birth
jokes.punchline: References Will's earlier description of being first person baby saw upside down
jokes.explanation: Sweet callback that shows Phil learned from Will's experience. Good character growth moment.

jokes.text: But because I want to be the first person our baby sees upside down.
jokes.setup: Phil explains why he wants to be in delivery room
jokes.punchline: He uses Will's exact phrase about being seen upside down
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing Will's influence on Phil's perspective change

jokes.text: But, I'm afraid, honey, that I'm gonna have to kill you, anyway.
jokes.setup: Vivian seems touched by Phil's beautiful sentiment
jokes.punchline: She's still going to kill him, referencing the murder mystery game
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to opening that brings the episode full circle. Good structure and timing.
